Brief Description

Experience Required: Junior DevOps Engineer with 0.5 - 3 years of experience CTC: 4-6 LPA Location: Trivandrum Work Mode: Work From Office

We are seeking a motivated and proactive Junior DevOps Engineer to support and enhance our cloud-native infrastructure and DevOps practices. This role involves working with modern cloud platforms, containerized environments, CI/CD pipelines, and monitoring systems to ensure high availability, scalability, and reliability of applications. The ideal candidate should be hands-on, eager to learn, collaborative, and passionate about automation, cloud technologies, and system reliability.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in building, deploying, and maintaining cloud infrastructure and containerized applications.
  • Work with AWS services (Fargate, ECS, DynamoDB, SNS, etc.) for managing distributed systems.
  • Monitor system performance using CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Splunk, and respond to alerts.
  • Support CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and ArgoCD.
  • Troubleshoot issues across infrastructure, networking, containers, and applications.
  • Assist in configuring and managing Kubernetes clusters, including deployments, services, and ingress.
  • Work on networking components such as load balancers, API gateways, reverse proxies, and service communication.
  • Participate in incident response, root cause analysis (RCA), and postmortems.
  • Automate repetitive tasks using scripts and tools to improve efficiency.
  • Maintain documentation for infrastructure, deployments, and troubleshooting processes.
  • Collaborate with development and operations teams to improve system reliability and deployment workflows.
  • Participate in on-call or shift rotations to ensure system availability.

Preferred Skills

Cloud Platforms and Services - Basic to intermediate knowledge of AWS services (ECS, Fargate, DynamoDB, SNS). - Basic to intermediate knowledge of GCP services (GKE, GAR, CloudSQL, Cloud Run). - Understanding of cloud-native architectures and distributed systems. - Familiarity with infrastructure provisioning concepts (Terraform/CloudFormation is a plus).

Monitoring and Logging Tools - Knowledge of CloudWatch Logs, Loki, Splunk for log analysis. - Familiarity with Prometheus and Grafana for metrics monitoring and dashboarding. - Understanding of alerting systems (Alertmanager) and incident response workflows. - Ability to analyze logs and metrics to identify system issues.

Networking and Security - Strong understanding of load balancers (e.g., ALB, NLB) for distributing traffic and troubleshooting connectivity issues. - Knowledge in API Gateways like AWS API Gateway or NGINX for managing API traffic. - Knowledge of reverse proxies and ingress controllers (e.g., NGINX Ingress, Traefik) for managing internal/external traffic. - Understanding service-to-service communications, including DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, and gRPC protocols. - Experience or hands-on knowledge with firewalls, security groups, and IAM roles for secure communications. - Troubleshooting skills for VM-related issues in platforms like AWS EC2 or equivalent.

DevOps Toolchains - Proficiency with Docker for managing container images and runtime debugging. - Strong understanding of Kubernetes concepts for managing deployments, ingress setups, and pod-related issues and troubleshooting. - Knowledge of CI/CD pipeline building tools (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD) for building, deploying, and managing automated pipelines. - Understanding of Ingress controllers (e.g., NGINX, Traefik) and SSL termination for secure routing.

Troubleshooting and Incident Management - Strong problem-solving skills to identify root causes using logs, metrics, and system-level debugging. - Ability to document detailed troubleshooting steps and solutions for recurring issues.

Collaboration and Communication - Ability to work with cross-functional teams (DevOps, development, and operations) to resolve incidents. - Skills in effective and proactive communication to escalate issues and provide updates during shift handovers. - Proficiency with tools like Slack, JIRA, Confluence, or Google Workspace for collaboration and issue tracking.

Introduction

We are seeking a 12+ years experienced Security Engineering Leader to drive enterprise security initiatives across DLP, PAM, password management, penetration testing, and access governance.

Responsibilities

  • Security Project Delivery:
  • Own the full lifecycle of enterprise security projects (charter, plan, execute, measure, close).

  • Drive outcomes through matrixed teams, ensuring accountability across stakeholders.

  • Data Loss Prevention (DLP):

  • Architect and deploy DLP policies for endpoints, email, cloud (M365/Purview), and network egress.
  • Define data classification schemas with Legal/Compliance/business leaders.
  • Tune DLP rules to minimize false positives while protecting PII, client data, and proprietary info.

  • Establish incident response workflows and executive reporting metrics.

  • Privileged Access Management (PAM):

  • Lead enterprise PAM platform rollout (e.g., CyberArk, BeyondTrust) and governance.
  • Define vaulting, session-recording, and just-in-time access policies for hybrid infrastructure.
  • Drive adoption with Infrastructure/Cloud/application teams.

  • Close PAM coverage gaps via structured roadmaps.

  • Password Management:

  • Design enterprise password strategies for workforce and privileged accounts (e.g., 1Password, Bitwarden).
  • Integrate tools with SSO, MFA, and identity governance platforms.

  • Embed credential security into onboarding/offboarding workflows.

  • Penetration Testing & Vulnerability Management:

  • Manage pentesting programs (vendor selection, scheduling, remediation tracking).
  • Coordinate red-team/purple-team exercises and translate findings into remediation roadmaps.
  • Validate remediation closure with Infrastructure/Application teams.

  • Produce executive-grade pentest summaries for CISO/CTO/board audiences.

  • Access Reviews & Identity Governance:

  • Design continuous access review programs for AD, Entra ID, and SaaS apps.
  • Automate workflows using IGA platforms (e.g., SailPoint, Saviynt).
  • Enforce RBAC/least-privilege principles with HR/Compliance/application owners.

  • Support audits (SOX, PCAOB) with evidence from access reviews.

  • Stakeholder Engagement:

  • Serve as the primary security liaison to business-unit leaders.
  • Present program status and risk posture to C-suite/board in non-technical language.
  • Collaborate with Legal/Compliance/HR/Finance to align with regulatory obligations.

Preferred Skills

  • Primary Skills:
  • 12+ years cybersecurity experience, including 5+ years in senior engineering/security lead roles.
  • Proven delivery of large-scale security projects on time and within budget.
  • Deep expertise in DLP, PAM, password management, pentesting, and access governance/IGA.
  • Experience in professional/financial services or regulated enterprises (5,000+ employees preferred).

  • Ability to influence cross-functional teams without direct authority.

  • Technical Proficiencies:

  • DLP: Microsoft Purview, Symantec DLP, Forcepoint.
  • PAM: CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea/Thycotic.
  • Identity/IGA: Entra ID (Azure AD), SailPoint, Saviynt, Okta.
  • Pentesting/Vulnerability Management: Rapid7, Tenable, NodeZero, Cobalt.
  • Microsoft 365/Azure Security Stack: Defender for Endpoint, Purview, Conditional Access.

  • Scripting: PowerShell, Python for security automation.

  • Certifications:

  • CISSP, CISM, or CRISC (strongly preferred).
